content:
# Corvia — Introduction Summary

## The Elevator Pitch (10 seconds)

> **Corvia is organizational reasoning memory for AI agents.** It's the knowledge layer that gives AI agents persistent, versioned, temporally queryable memory — so they stop forgetting everything between sessions.

## The Short Version (1 minute)

Every AI coding agent today — Goose, Cursor, Claude Code, Copilot — has amnesia. Each session starts from scratch. Your team's decisions, context, and institutional knowledge evaporate the moment the session ends.

**Corvia fixes that.**

It's an open-source knowledge layer (written in Rust) that sits *underneath* AI agents and gives them:

- **Persistent memory** — knowledge survives across sessions, agents, and teams
- **Temporal awareness** — "what did we know last week?" is a real query
- **Branch-aware reasoning** — parallel workstreams with isolated knowledge that merges intelligently
- **Multi-agent coordination** — multiple agents read and write concurrently without corrupting each other
- **Git as source of truth** — all knowledge is version-controlled and rebuildable

Think of it as **Redis for AI agent memory** — infrastructure that makes every agent framework smarter.

## The Technical Summary (3 minutes)

**Architecture:**
```
Integration Surface:  REST API  ·  MCP Server  ·  Rust crate
                              │
Frontends:           CLI  ·  VS Code Extension
                              │
Adapters:            Git/Code (tree-sitter)  ·  General  ·  Community
                              │
Kernel:              Knowledge Store  ·  Agent Coordinator
                     Embedding Pipeline  ·  Context Builder
                     Merge Worker
                              │
Storage:             Git (truth)  ·  SurrealDB (query)  ·  Redb (coord)
```

**Five kernel subsystems:**

| Subsystem | What it does |
|-----------|-------------|
| **Knowledge Store** | Bi-temporal read/write against SurrealDB — vector search, graph traversal, temporal queries |
| **Agent Coordinator** | Session lifecycle, ephemeral namespaces, write isolation via Redb |
| **Embedding Pipeline** | Provider-agnostic (local-first via Ollama/vLLM), incremental embed-on-write |
| **Context Builder** | The isolation enforcer — ensures agents only see what they're authorized to see |
| **Merge Worker** | Conflict resolution via LLM-assisted merge (the AI resolves its own conflicts) |

**Three storage layers:**
- **Git** — source of truth for all knowledge. `corvia rebuild` reconstructs everything from Git.
- **SurrealDB** — unified queryable store (vectors + graphs + time-series in one Docker service)
- **Redb** — embedded Rust coordination (agent sessions, merge queue, branch registry)

**Three integration paths:**
1. `cargo add corvia` — Rust crate, in-process, zero network hop
2. `corvia serve` → REST API at `:8020` — OpenAI-compatible, any language
3. `corvia serve --mcp` → MCP server — native for Claude and agent frameworks

**Key design principles:**
- **Local-first** — works with $0 and no API keys (Ollama/vLLM default)
- **Provider-agnostic** — any LLM, any embedding model
- **Domain-agnostic kernel** — adapters bring domain knowledge (Git adapter is v1)
- **AGPL v3** — fully open source with SaaS protection, dual-license path

## The "Why Should I Care" Version (for developers)

You're already using AI agents in your workflow. But every time you start a new session, you're re-explaining your architecture, your decisions, your conventions. Your agents don't *know* your codebase — they read files on demand and forget immediately.

Corvia turns that from:
```
Agent reads code → answers question → forgets everything
```
Into:
```
Agent reads code → writes knowledge to Corvia → next agent reads from Corvia
                   → knowledge compounds → agents get smarter over time
```

Your team's AI agents share a brain. Decisions made in one session inform the next. Temporal queries let you ask "what changed between the v2.0 release and now?" Knowledge is branch-aware, so your feature branch agents don't pollute main.

## The "Why Now" Version (for investors/ecosystem)

The AI agent space is exploding — Meta acquired Manus for $2B, Goose has 27K GitHub stars, every IDE has an AI copilot. But the entire ecosystem shares one critical gap: **agents have no memory infrastructure.**

Every agent framework today either:
- Stores nothing (session-only, most tools)
- Stores flat context in vector DBs (mem0, Zep — no temporal, no branching, no multi-agent)
- Relies on the user to manually maintain context

Corvia is the **missing infrastructure layer** — purpose-built for the world where agents are the primary consumers of organizational knowledge, not humans.

## Market Positioning

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    THE AI AGENT STACK                        │
│                                                             │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  AGENTS (what users interact with)                    │  │
│  │  • Manus, Goose, Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot        │  │
│  └──────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                         │ agents need memory                │
│  ┌──────────────────────▼────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  MEMORY LAYER  ◄── THIS IS CORVIA                    │  │
│  │  • Organizational reasoning memory                    │  │
│  │  • Temporal queries, branch-aware, multi-agent        │  │
│  │  • Versioned knowledge with semantic diff             │  │
│  └──────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                         │                                   │
│  ┌──────────────────────▼────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │  INFERENCE (LLM providers)                            │  │
│  │  • OpenAI, Anthropic, vLLM, Ollama                    │  │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Corvia doesn't compete with agents — it makes them better. The pitch isn't "use Corvia instead of Goose." It's "use Corvia *with* Goose (or any agent) and your agents will finally remember."
